topic: 伝統食 知られざる
query: Tell me about Japan's traditional food culture that hasn't gone mainstream yet.
reasoning: RULE B applied: single root cause is constraint failure — the query's defining constraint ('hasn't gone mainstream') is entirely ignored. No keyword or ranking filter for obscure/regional items is applied; the 500 returned items are sorted by registration number, surfacing internationally famous products (Yubari Melon, Tajima Beef) that are the opposite of 'not mainstream'. Intent and practical_usefulness each drop by 1 from their otherwise-deserved scores due to the same root cause (Rule B: no additional dim compound). Groundedness and factual_accuracy are 5 because all records are real MAFF GI official data. Specificity is 3 (authoritative data present but scope/filter gap per Rule C). Expression quality is 4 (well-formed JSON with helpful truncation_note).
missing_data: Filtering or ranking for lesser-known / regional / non-internationally-famous GI items; keyword narrowing for '知られざる' / 'not mainstream'; ideally a ranked subset of items with low international recognition scores or from rural/minor prefectures.
improvement_hint: Add a 'popularity_tier' or 'obscurity_flag' field to food items (e.g. derived from Wikidata sitelink count or scrape mention frequency) so the tool can filter for lesser-known entries when the intent classifier detects 'hidden/unknown/not mainstream' queries; alternatively expose a 'keyword' arg so agents can pass 'regional' or prefecture codes to narrow the 22864-item corpus before truncation.
